facts.text: Lake Chaffee was created before 1934 when an earthen dam was built across Chaffee Brook, turning a wetland into a 54‑acre lake.
The lake’s average depth is just 6 feet, but its deepest spot reaches 11 feet, making it a shallow, shallow‑water retreat for residents.
Lake Chaffee’s shoreline stretches about 8,400 feet, excluding its two islands, giving plenty of waterfront for the roughly 260 property owners.
Water exchange in Lake Chaffee takes nearly six months, a cycle that keeps the lake’s ecosystem fresh and its fish populations healthy.
The lake sits at an elevation of 856 feet above sea level, a modest rise that gives the surrounding homes a gentle overlook of the water.
Lake Chaffee’s watershed covers about 284 acres, feeding the lake with runoff from intermittent streams that flow into Chaffee Brook and then the Mount Hope River.
